title: How do Germans cope with having Nazi grandparents?
summary: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

title: Ukraine denies involvement in Nord Stream blasts
summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general proposed forming a joint investigative team with Germany. Berlin has indicted a former Ukrainian soldier over the blasts and says he was working on behalf of Ukrainian entities.

title: Germany news: Man jailed over killing of train conductor
summary: A verdict has been delivered in a trial that drew national attention and raised concern about train staff safety. Meanwhile, VW bosses are eyeing huge restructuring, with unions vowing to fight any closures. DW has more.

title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

title: Germany: June heat wave linked to 5,000 excess deaths
summary: Several other European Union countries reported thousands of additional deaths as June 2026 officially became the hottest on record for bloc.

title: East German doping victims still lack adequate support
summary: Thousands of athletes in former East Germany were subjected to state-sponsored doping — and many still suffer lasting health effects. But support for victims is not enough, German commissioner says.

title: Germany: Police arrest suspect after Bavaria school attack
summary: Authorities say several people have been injured following a reported attack at a school in southern Germany. Two girls were said to have been badly hurt at the school, where a teenage suspect was arrested.

title: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
summary: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

title: Are German companies leaving the country?
summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

title: Germany reports 'shock' rise in drug deaths among under-30s
summary: The number of drug-related deaths remains at a record high in Germany. And the victims are getting younger and younger. Many deaths are related to new and increasingly dangerous substances such as synthetic opioids.
